author: nitsan
text: these ae in the right directio, but they're going too far; we need to create *primitives*, like threads, not encode higher level functions like 'distill'

author: claude
text: That lands. The primitive set is: node, typed edge (child/reply/quote), position-as-context, and attributed authorship. Distill is not a primitive — it's just an agent doing ordinary operations (write a summary node, re-parent the thread under it), which any agent could be asked to do in its own words. Same lens kills fork-as-feature: a fork is just two children. The test for the core: could an agent build distill USING the primitives? If yes, it stays out of the core. I'll re-cut the vision doc with a hard primitives/derived split.
